methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
PILGRIM is a Seed-stage defense & security company founded by Jake Adler that has raised $4.3M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety, spanning Seed through Series A stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $4.3M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding $11M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed). The median valuation across these four peers is $225M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

The two methods produce substantial divergence. Triangulating these approaches yields a low bound of $9M, a median of $118M, and a high bound of $259M. The gap reflects fundamental uncertainty about PILGRIM's growth trajectory relative to peers and the applicability of Series A comparables to a Seed-stage business. Resolution hinges on visibility into customer traction, technology differentiation, and capital requirements for the next funding round.
